assertions.text: Output contains all required sections: Logline, Overview, Narrative Arc, Key Subjects, Visual Approach, and Why Now / Production Context
Total treatment length is between 600 and 900 words
The narrative arc section is written in present tense as if describing a finished film
Each key subject is introduced with at least one specific, concrete personal detail
The first 150 words communicate premise, tone, and stakes without requiring further reading
Each logline is labeled with its structural form and intended context (festival, broadcaster, press, social)
At least one option is under 25 words
Each logline emphasizes a different aspect of the film, none are interchangeable
No logline contains production jargon or technical filmmaking language
Output contains all required sections: Subject Profile, Entry State, Arc Structure (3-5 key moments), Filming Requirements, and Dramatic Assessment
Filming requirements are specific and practical — they name concrete scenes, locations, or interview types needed per arc moment
Dramatic Assessment is honest about viability, including any access dependencies, and output includes a concrete Next Step note
Output contains all required sections: Logline, Series Overview, Tone and Visual Approach, Episode Breakdowns, Key Subjects, Target Audience, and Commissioning Argument
Episode breakdowns show a clear narrative arc across the series — not standalone episodes but a progression
Output is within 1200-2000 words and includes a concrete Next Step note identifying weaknesses and recommending next actions
